text.title: xUnit Testing Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding xUnit + Moq + WebApplicationFactory conventions on .NET 8.

rule.statement: Every async test method MUST return `Task` (or `ValueTask`). `async void` is forbidden except for legacy event-handler tests.
rule.rationale: `async void` exceptions are unobserved by the xUnit runner; failures pass silently or crash the runner.

rule.statement: Controller integration tests MUST use `IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>` (or a derived test factory) and override `ConfigureWebHost` to substitute `DbContext` with SQLite-in-memory or Testcontainers. Production database/external APIs MUST NOT be touched.
rule.rationale: `IClassFixture` shares the factory across tests in the class, amortizing host-startup cost. Substituting DbContext keeps tests deterministic.

rule.statement: Parametric tests MUST use `[Theory]` + `[InlineData(...)]` (or `[MemberData]`/`[ClassData]` for complex types), not duplicated `[Fact]` methods. Test name MUST include the input/expected output: `Add_TwoPositives_ReturnsSum`.
rule.rationale: `[Theory]` reports each case as a separate test result; duplicated `[Fact]`s hide which case failed.

rule.statement: Moq `Setup` arguments MUST use precise matchers (`It.Is<T>(x => x.Id == 1)`) by default. `It.IsAny<T>()` is allowed only when the test explicitly does not care about the argument.
rule.rationale: `It.IsAny<T>()` masks divergence between setup and test code; tests pass even when the code calls the mock with wrong args.

rule.statement: All assertions on non-primitive types (objects, collections, JSON responses) MUST use `FluentAssertions` (`.Should().BeEquivalentTo(...)`). `Assert.Equal` on objects is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `Assert.Equal` produces a single-line `Expected: X; Actual: Y` dump; `Should().BeEquivalentTo` produces a multiline structural diff.
